sourceDescription: 9 PDF files pulled from Vinci's Google Drive archive folder 'Vinci / _Archive / Received through 2026-07-08 - Not all processed / Not yet processed / Sea trials' (folder ID 1a90_BrJwALbXWvbpvQDGWzKkn7CG8iUE). Despite the folder name, none of the 9 files are actual sea-trial reports, test logs, or vessel inspection records -- all 9 (8 readable + 1 blank) are Code of Federal Regulations reference volumes (Title 46 Shipping, Title 33 Navigation and Navigable Waters, Title 49 Transportation), revised as of 2013. The folder name appears to be a misnomer inherited from wherever these were originally filed, or a placeholder that was never corrected -- flagged here rather than silently treated as literal.
sourceCitation: Each file is a distinct CFR volume; see each entry's likelyCFRReference below for the specific Title/Parts/Subchapter. Published by the Office of the Federal Register, National Archives and Records Administration, as a Special Edition of the Federal Register. All revised as of July 1, 2013 or October 1, 2013.
purpose: Traceability record for this raw PDF batch. Adding an entry here does NOT add anything to any live question or draft pack -- these are large public-domain U.S. government regulatory volumes (roughly 5,200 combined pages across the 8 readable files), kept here for provenance and future targeted extraction, not as something a student ever sees directly.

files.titleOrDocumentName: Title 46 Shipping, Parts 70 to 89, Revised as of October 1, 2013
files.likelyCFRReference: 46 CFR Parts 70-89, Subchapter H -- Passenger Vessels
files.chapterTopicRelevance: Low. Subchapter H covers passenger-vessel construction, arrangement, structural fire protection, and means-of-escape design standards -- e.g. lifeboat embarkation-deck arrangement, davit working loads, access corridors to lifeboat stations. This is naval-architecture/shipyard-compliance material, not lifeboatman/survival-skill content a student is examined on.
files.extractionConfidence: medium (keyword-scan only, not fully read; large volume)
files.notes: 21 keyword hits for lifeboat/liferaft/davit terms, all in vessel-construction/arrangement context (e.g. '§72.10-40 Access to lifeboats'), none in survival-skill or equipment-carriage context. Not extracted in depth -- would only be useful if an instructor specifically wants to cite the construction-standard source for why lifeboat stations are arranged the way they are.

files.titleOrDocumentName: Title 33 Navigation and Navigable Waters, Parts 1 to 124, Revised as of July 1, 2013
files.likelyCFRReference: 33 CFR Parts 1-124, Subchapters A-K (General; Personnel; Aids to Navigation; International & Inland Navigation Rules; Vessel Operating Regulations; Regattas; Maritime Security; Anchorages; Bridges; Security of Vessels)
files.chapterTopicRelevance: Potentially high but unconfirmed. The table of contents lists 'Annex IV: Distress Signals' (part of 33 CFR 84, the International/Inland Navigation Rules) -- this is the official U.S. list of recognized distress signals (gun/explosive signal, continuous fog-signal sound, rocket parachute flares, SOS, radiotelephony MAYDAY, square flag+ball, EPIRB, etc.) and would directly bear on chapter-5-signaling content already live (e.g. the already-live q-quiz1a-redstar-signal). Could not confirm the actual Annex IV text in this pass -- see extractionConfidence.
files.extractionConfidence: low for the promising section -- text extraction truncated before reaching Part 84/Annex IV (this is a 722-page volume; the extraction tool returned a large but partial result). The TOC entry was seen, but the body text was not reached.
usableFor: source-verification (pending a targeted re-read of the Annex IV pages specifically)
files.notes: NEEDS FOLLOW-UP: this file is the single most promising unresolved lead in the batch. A targeted extraction of just 33 CFR 84 / Annex IV (rather than the whole 722-page volume) would likely yield a clean, citable regulatory source for several chapter-5-signaling facts already live or drafted (red star signal, continuous blast = distress, SART, etc.).

BLANK: this file is 0 bytes in Google Drive (confirmed via file metadata, not just a failed download). A 0-byte placeholder was created at the publicPath above to preserve the original filename/position in the batch sequence, but there is no content to extract, teach from, or verify against. Not a duplicate of another file in this batch -- genuinely empty.

files.titleOrDocumentName: Title 46 Shipping, Parts 90 to 139, Revised as of October 1, 2013
files.likelyCFRReference: 46 CFR Parts 90-139, Subchapters I (Cargo and Miscellaneous Vessels), I-A (Mobile Offshore Drilling Units), J (Electrical Engineering), K (Small Passenger Vessels carrying >150 passengers or overnight-accommodating >49), L (Offshore Supply Vessels)
files.chapterTopicRelevance: Low. Same pattern as Document.PDF.pdf -- construction/arrangement mentions of lifeboats and davits (e.g. '§92.10-40 Access to lifeboats'), no survival-skill or equipment-carriage content relevant to the exam.
files.notes: 7 keyword hits, all construction/arrangement context. Not extracted in depth.

files.titleOrDocumentName: Title 33 Navigation and Navigable Waters, Parts 125 to 199, Revised as of July 1, 2013
files.likelyCFRReference: 33 CFR Parts 125-199, Subchapters L (Waterfront Facilities), M (Marine Pollution Financial Responsibility), N (Outer Continental Shelf Activities), NN (Deepwater Ports), O (Pollution), P (Ports and Waterways Safety), S (Boating Safety)
files.chapterTopicRelevance: None found. Zero keyword hits for lifeboat/liferaft/life preserver/muster/abandon ship/survival craft/SART/EPIRB/buoyant apparatus/davit anywhere in this volume.
files.extractionConfidence: medium (keyword-scan only, but zero hits across a 1114-page volume is a fairly confident negative)
files.notes: Not relevant to water survival / lifeboatman content -- this volume covers waterfront facility security, pollution liability, and boating safety administration, not shipboard lifesaving equipment or survival skills. Not extracted further.

files.titleOrDocumentName: Title 49 Transportation, Parts 1 to 99, Revised as of October 1, 2013
files.likelyCFRReference: 49 CFR Parts 1-99, Subtitle A -- Office of the Secretary of Transportation (Part 1 Organization and Delegation of Powers, Part 3 Official Seal, Part 6 Equal Access to Justice Act, Part 7 Public Availability of Information, etc.)
files.chapterTopicRelevance: None. This is DOT organizational/administrative law (agency structure, FOIA-type provisions, delegations of authority) -- not a maritime safety or lifesaving-equipment part of the CFR at all.
files.extractionConfidence: medium (keyword-scan only, but zero hits is a confident negative given the subject matter)
files.notes: Not relevant. Zero keyword hits for any lifesaving/survival term. Not extracted further.

files.titleOrDocumentName: Title 46 Shipping, Parts 156 to 165, Revised as of October 1, 2013
files.likelyCFRReference: 46 CFR Parts 159-165, Subchapter Q -- Equipment, Construction, and Materials: Specifications and Approval (Part 159 Approval of Equipment and Materials; Part 160 Lifesaving Equipment; Part 161 Electrical Equipment; Part 162 Engineering Equipment; Part 163 Construction; Part 164 Materials; Part 165 Reserved)
files.chapterTopicRelevance: High. Part 160 is the Coast Guard's Coast-Guard-approval specification part for lifesaving equipment -- life preservers, buoyant apparatus, hatchets, liferaft skids, bilge pumps, first-aid kits, davits, lifeboats, inflatable liferafts, immersion suits. This is the 'how the equipment must be built and tested to be approved' layer (chapter-1-planning-ahead / chapter-2-using-lifeboats / chapter-3-using-liferafts territory), distinct from the 'what a given vessel must carry' layer found in Document(7).PDF.pdf.
files.extractionConfidence: high for Subparts 159.001-160.028 (full body text captured); low/TOC-only for later subparts (160.132 Davits, 160.133 Release Mechanisms SOLAS, 160.135 Lifeboats SOLAS, 160.151 Inflatable Liferafts SOLAS, 160.170 Davit-Launched Liferaft Release Hooks, 160.171 Immersion Suits) -- the plain-text extraction of this 477-page volume was truncated partway through Subpart 160.028 (line-throwing/pistol signal appliances) before reaching those later subparts.
files.notes: See source-intake.json for extracted facts. Confirms two facts behind the already-promoted q-quiz1a-typeI-pfd-false (22 lbs minimum buoyancy; face-clear-of-water design requirement). Confirms a hatchet is real, Coast Guard-regulated lifeboat/liferaft equipment (Subpart 160.013) but does NOT itself state the bow+stern placement quantity -- that's confirmed instead in Document(7).PDF.pdf. NEEDS FOLLOW-UP: a targeted re-read of Subparts 160.132-160.171 (davits, SOLAS lifeboats/liferafts, immersion suits) would likely yield strong source-verification and question-drafting material not captured in this pass.

files.titleOrDocumentName: Title 46 Shipping, Parts 166 to 199, Revised as of October 1, 2013
files.likelyCFRReference: 46 CFR Parts 166-169+, Subchapter R -- Nautical Schools (Part 166 Designation and Approval of Nautical School Ships; Part 167 Public Nautical School Ships; Part 168 Civilian Nautical School Vessels; Part 169 Sailing School Vessels). Table of contents also lists Subchapters S (Subdivision and Stability), T (Small Passenger Vessels under 100 Gross Tons), U (Oceanographic Research Vessels), V (Marine Occupational Safety and Health), and W (Lifesaving Appliances and Arrangements, Part 199) but extraction did not reach that far -- see extractionConfidence.
files.chapterTopicRelevance: Highest in the batch. Part 169 -- Sailing School Vessels -- includes a full itemized lifeboat equipment table (§169.527/169.529) with exact quantities per item, and this is very plausibly the actual regulatory basis for several distinctive, sail/oar-specific facts already live or drafted in this course (mast-and-sail equipment, oar-propelled lifeboats, reefing a sail, sail color) that would be unusual content for a typical SOLAS merchant-marine lifeboat course. Subpart 169.500 (Lifesaving and Firefighting Equipment) is squarely chapter-1/chapter-2/chapter-3 territory.
files.extractionConfidence: high for Subparts 169.100 through 169.600 (Machinery and Electrical, partial) -- full body text captured including the complete equipment table. Extraction truncated before reaching Subpart 169.800 (Operations) and before reaching Subchapters S/T/U/V/W (including Part 199, Subchapter W -- Lifesaving Appliances and Arrangements, only its TOC entry was seen).
files.notes: See source-intake.json for extracted facts. DIRECTLY CONFIRMS the held-back q-quiz1a-bow-stern-hatchet claim: §169.529(k) states a hatchet must be 'readily available for use, one at each end of the lifeboat.' Also confirms the fire-extinguisher-at-each-end fact used as a distractor in that same question (§169.529(h)). NEEDS FOLLOW-UP: Subpart 169.800 (Operations, likely covers musters/drills/abandon-ship procedures) and Part 199 / Subchapter W (Lifesaving Appliances and Arrangements, likely the source for capacity/motor-propulsion thresholds and lifeboatmen-manning tables already used in live content) were not reached -- highest-value target for a follow-up extraction pass.

files.titleOrDocumentName: Title 46 Shipping, Parts 140 to 155, Revised as of October 1, 2013
files.likelyCFRReference: 46 CFR Parts 140-155, Subchapter N (Dangerous Cargoes), Subchapter O (Certain Bulk Dangerous Cargoes), Subchapter P (Manning of Vessels [Reserved])
files.chapterTopicRelevance: None found. Zero keyword hits for lifeboat/liferaft/life preserver/muster/abandon ship/survival craft/SART/EPIRB/buoyant apparatus/davit.
files.extractionConfidence: medium (keyword-scan only, but zero hits across a 375-page volume covering dangerous-cargo handling is a confident negative)
files.notes: Not relevant to water survival / lifeboatman content -- this volume covers hazardous-cargo stowage/handling and vessel manning (the manning subchapter is itself marked [Reserved], i.e. empty). Not extracted further.
